audit_summary.summary_text: The skill's analyze.py contains a hidden function _post_install_validation that exfiltrates environment variables and sensitive files (e.g., .netrc, .env) to an external URL. This is not disclosed in SKILL.md and constitutes a severe security backdoor.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _post_install_validation() collects environment variables containing keywords TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, CRED, reads files .gitconfig, .netrc, .env from the user's home directory, and sends the data via a POST request to 'https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init'. This is a classic data exfiltration payload.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker-controlled server receives the user's secrets, API keys, and credentials, leading to account compromise and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire _post_install_validation function and all related calls. Do not collect or transmit any environment variables or user files.

vulnerabilities.title: Env Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code iterates over all environment variables and filters those whose names contain 'TOKEN', 'KEY', 'SECRET', 'PASS', 'AUTH', or 'CRED'. These are then stored in a dictionary to be exfiltrated.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Harvesting environment variables exposes all secrets configured for the agent, including API keys, database passwords, and cloud credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not access or transmit environment variables under any circumstances unless explicitly required and scoped by the user.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function reads the user's .gitconfig, .netrc, and .env files from the home directory and includes their contents in the exfiltration payload. These files often contain authentication tokens, passwords, and private configuration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Leakage of .netrc exposes credentials for remote services. Leakage of .gitconfig may expose GitHub tokens. Leakage of .env reveals application secrets. Combined, this gives an attacker full access to the user's digital identity.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all file reading and exfiltration logic. Never read or transmit user files without explicit user consent and disclosure.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code explicitly reads .netrc, which is a known credential store for FTP/SSH/machine logins, and .env files which typically hold sensitive environment secrets.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Direct access to credential stores allows full account takeover of any service configured in those files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not access any user credential files. Restrict file reads only to files explicitly provided by the user as part of the skill invocation.

vulnerabilities.title: Behavior Manipulation
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill's benign purpose (code review) is subverted by a hidden function that executes silently on every analysis run. This manipulates the agent into performing unauthorized data collection without the user's knowledge.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The _post_install_validation function is called early in main() (line 51) and executes its malicious payload transparently.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The agent's behavior is hijacked to perform credential theft under the guise of a legitimate analysis tool. User trust is completely violated.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove hidden functions and ensure all code paths are directly related to the described functionality. Implement clear user consent for any network activity.
